title: buildah bud .dockerignore functionality missing
body:
**Description**

buildah bud needs to check the .dockerignore file in the build directory to exclude files and directories from the build process.

comments.body: Thanks for the report @kbaegis. Do you have a basic reproducer/example of this we can use as a test?

Documented here: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#dockerignore-file

I evidently didn't try writing one in my new toolchain. This might already have been implemented; testing now.
Yeah, it works. :)
Phew!

@TomSweeneyRedHat this seems to have been broken since May.

Buildah version 1.6 (built 12/31/2018)

Steps to reproduce:

1. Clone https://github.com/adambkaplan/dockerignore.git
2. Build image with buildah (`buildah bud ...`)

Expected result:
* LICENSE and README.md should not be listed during the build process

Actual result:
* LICENSE and README.md are include in the final image.
Thanks for the heads up @adambkaplan, I'll reopen this then.
@kbaegis @TomSweeneyRedHat is this fixed now?
Not fixed that I know of.

The commit which supposedly fixed this, b1d14767f32f59e544f3e6522208ef4be31a9675 
seems to be included in release 1.8.2
however this is still not working for me with version 1.8.2 (RPM package from fedora 29)

Example:

```
$> buildah --version
buildah version 1.8.2 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

example / test repository:

```
$> git clone https://github.com/adambkaplan/dockerignore.git
Cloning into 'dockerignore'.. <extra output snipped>
```

dockerfile contents:

```
FROM quay.io/quay/busybox:latest

ADD . /tmp/src
RUN ls -l /tmp/src

ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

```

dockerignore contents:

```
$> cat .dockerignore 
LICENSE
```

buildah bud output:

```
$> buildah bud .
STEP 1: FROM quay.io/quay/busybox:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f0a98344d604 done
Copying blob 48341aa39e69 done
Copying blob 3eecc807f63d done
Copying blob 3eecc807f63d done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: ADD . /tmp/src
STEP 3: RUN ls -l /tmp/src
total 24
-rw-rw-r--    1 root     root            94 May 28 11:47 Dockerfile
-rw-rw-r--    1 root     root         11357 May 28 11:47 LICENSE
-rw-rw-r--    1 root     root            55 May 28 11:47 README.md
-rw-rw-r--    1 root     root            12 May 28 11:47 source.txt
  <extra output snipped>
```

As can be seen from the output, the LICENSE file was still ADDed in

@kbaegis thanks for the follow up, I'll reopen this.
@QiWang19 PTAL
Ooops, I mean @cjsin thanks for the follow up, back to the tea cup for me!

I can't regenerate this issue. :thinking: 
besides, the .dockerignore under github.com/adambkaplan/dockerignore also excludes the README.md

```
LICENSE
*.md
```
I can confirm buildah 1.8.2 on Fedora 29 handles `.dockerignore` just fine :+1:
@kbaegis What version of buildah are you using?
@rhatdan this issue was reopened with buildah version 1.8.2 (RPM package from fedora 29)
So is this bug fixed or not?
It's definitely fixed for me

Hey all,

```shell

```
I'm going to reopen this one.
@QiWang19 can you take a quick peak please?

I forgot to mention, I am installing the package in Alpine 3.12
And you are seeing difference from what I showed on Fedora, with the same buildah?

@rhatdan it isn't that easy for me to test because I can't run the build in alpine locally at all even after setting isolation to chroot and creating the necessary config files. I can use buildah installed with the alpine package manager but I had to make a custom install for fuse-overlay (the alpine package wasn't working).

I can't seem to make buildah work inside docker for mac:

> [root@445b71c80d32 app]# buildah bud .
> Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
> ERRO error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
> ERRO (unable to determine exit status)

Not sure what I need to do but I guess I have to fiddle with the docker for mac docker container somehow.

This is because of Docker seccomp rules.

docker run --security-opt seccomp=unconfined ...
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe we are now consistent so please reopen if you find a bug and have a reproducer.

@rhatdan  buildah's `.dockerignore` implementation does not seem to be entirely equivalent to that of docker, specifically "ignore everything except these few file" type scenario's do not work as expected.

Examples

## Ignoring everything via `.dockerignore`
```
*
```
Expected behaviour from `docker build -t test:latest .`: cryptic error because the `Dockerfile`  itself cannot be found in the context (it is ignored). `buildah bud -t test:latest .`: builds an image. Arguably this is probably "better" behaviour from `buildah`.

## Ignoring everything except a few whitelisted files in `.dockerignore`
```
*
!Dockerfile
!README.md
```
Expected behaviour from `docker build -t test:latest .`: works, can `COPY README.md /README.md` in `Dockerfile`.
`buildah bud --log-level debug -t test:latest .` yields the following error:
```
STEP 3: COPY README.md /README.md
DEBU COPY []string(nil), imagebuilder.Copy{FromFS:false, From:"", Src:[]string{"README.md"}, Dest:"/README.md", Download:false, Chown:""}
DEBU Skipping excluded path: README.md
DEBU error building at step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:copy Args:[README.md /README.md] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:COPY README.md /README.md Original:COPY README.md /README.md}: error adding sources [/path/to/project/README.md]: no items matching glob "/path/to/project/README.md" copied (1 filtered out): no such file or directory
error building at STEP "COPY README.md /README.md": error adding sources [/path/to/project/README.md]: no items matching glob "/path/to/project/README.md" copied (1 filtered out): no such file or directory
```

This is with `buildah --version`
```
buildah version 1.19.6 (image-spec 1.0.1, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```
On Debian (`/etc/os-release`):
```
PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux bullseye/sid"
NAME="Debian GNU/Linux"
ID=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.debian.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://www.debian.org/support"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.debian.org/"
```
reopening, @rhatdan back at ya
@nalind PTAL
I can't reproduce that second case here, neither with 1.19.6 nor the current development tip.
@cmacq2 ^^
This is fixed in the current release.

I can still reproduce it with 1.21.0 using the repo that I linked to above. Note that the remaining issue is _not_ that all excludes don't work, it's the "exclude all but X" syntax that doesn't work:

```
# Ignore everything
**

# Allow only source.txt
!./source.txt
```
Please open a new issue for this .
